meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve can't deploy apps (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome can't deploy apps. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Git with AI: Overcome Deployment Blocks (Advanced Guide)
content.seo_description: Master Git with AI to solve deployment hurdles. A high-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your student portfolio projects.
content.answer_block: Deployments fail when environment configurations drift from code logic. By integrating AI-driven Git workflows with Antigravity and Claude Code, you automate infrastructure mapping and resolve dependency conflicts in real-time. This approach turns manual, error-prone deployment attempts into a streamlined, version-controlled pipeline that ensures your code runs exactly as intended.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks architecting your first SaaS application. The logic is sound, the UI is polished, and the code works flawlessly on localhost. Yet, the moment you push to production, the screen goes dark. Deployment isn't just a hurdle; it is the graveyard of ambitious student projects, turning hours of creative coding into a frustrating cycle of environment variables, missing dependencies, and silent server crashes.

But the era of manual debugging is over. By leveraging Git as your source of truth and AI as your infrastructure engineer, you can bypass the 'it works on my machine' nightmare. We are moving beyond basic commits; we are using AI to bridge the gap between local development and production-ready architecture.

steps.title: Architecting the Deployment Manifest
steps.description: Before pushing, ensure your project structure is ready for the cloud by using AI to generate a robust Dockerfile and environment schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project structure and generate a production-ready Dockerfile and a .env.example file. Ensure all necessary build arguments and runtime dependencies are explicitly defined for a Node.js/Python environment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate production-ready Dockerfile and env schema based on project files'

steps.title: Automated Version Control Hygiene
steps.description: Clean up your repository to ensure that only deployable code reaches the production branch, preventing environment noise.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the repository for unnecessary build artifacts and secret exposures. Create a .gitignore file that prevents local environment clutter from leaking into Git.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Audit repo for secrets and cleanup .gitignore'

steps.title: Configuring the CI/CD Pipeline
steps.description: Use AI to write a GitHub Actions workflow that automates testing and deployment triggers on every push.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a GitHub Actions workflow file that runs unit tests and executes a deployment script only if tests pass. Include logic for environment variable injection.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline for automated testing and deployment'

steps.title: Resolving Deployment Dependency Conflicts
steps.description: Use Claude Code to trace dependency versions that cause production crashes compared to your local environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Compare local node_modules or pip environment with production specs. Identify version discrepancies that could cause runtime failures.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Identify and resolve dependency version mismatches between local and production'

steps.title: Final Production Validation
steps.description: Perform a dry-run deployment simulation to catch errors before they hit your live URL.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Simulate a build environment using the Dockerfile generated in step 1. Check for any missing environment variables or runtime errors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Perform dry-run deployment validation and report potential runtime errors'

pro_tips: Always use a .env.example file; never commit real secrets to Git, even in private repos.
Tag your production releases in Git so you can instantly roll back if a deployment fails.
Use AI to write your README.md; clear documentation often reveals architectural gaps you missed.
Set up a 'staging' branch that mirrors production to test deployments before pushing to main.
Treat your infrastructure as code; if it isn't in your Git repo, it doesn't exist in production.
content.next_steps: Once your deployment is live, integrate monitoring tools like Sentry to catch runtime errors, and begin documenting your project on your portfolio site to showcase your professional dev workflow.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical edge to deploy any project with confidence. Stop letting deployment errors hold back your potential as a software engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
